f7aa964bd0ddfbc6526a167aa98ada06b1dff1a1.title: Equal-Spacing Amplification Bound
f7aa964bd0ddfbc6526a167aa98ada06b1dff1a1.body:
For every integer $\beta\ge 1$ and every $q\in(0,1]$, let
\[
p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,q)_j=\frac{qj}{\beta},
\qquad j=0,\ldots,\beta .
\]
Then $p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,q)_j\in[0,1]$ for every $j=0,\ldots,\beta$, and, with $A_\beta$ as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:amplification-criterion},
\[
A_\beta\!\left(p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,q)\right)
\le
9\left(\frac{\beta}{q}\right)^{2\beta}.
\]

8ddc4f417c7ab0dc47c4e1aaa44c0fa3dfc567ee.title: Full-Budget Endpoint Rule
8ddc4f417c7ab0dc47c4e1aaa44c0fa3dfc567ee.body:
For every polynomial order \(\beta\ge 1\), take \(k=\beta\) and use the equal-spacing schedule \(p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,1)\). Define the weight vector \(w=(w_0,\ldots,w_\beta)\) by
\[
w_0=-1,\qquad w_\beta=1,\qquad w_j=0 \quad\text{for } 1\le j\le \beta-1 .
\]
Then \(w\in W_\beta(p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,1))\), where \(W_\beta(\cdot)\) is the unbiased weight set in Definition~\ref{obj:def:unbiased-weight-set}. Consequently, for the amplification criterion in Definition~\ref{obj:def:amplification-criterion},
\[
A_\beta\!\left(p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,1)\right)\le 4 .
\]

b8cd92e899af80e658f1b3a845270638d5234886.title: Rollout Polynomial Identity
b8cd92e899af80e658f1b3a845270638d5234886.body:
For any finite assignment space $\Omega$, population size $n$, number of rollout intervals $k$, polynomial order $\beta$, contemporaneous assignment vectors $Z_j$, potential outcomes $Y_i(Z_j)$, round means $\bar Y_j$, rollout mean curve $m_P$, and polynomial coefficients $(a_{P,\ell})_{\ell\ge 0}$, suppose Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:static-rollout-consistency} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:beta-order-polynomial} hold. Then
\[
m_P(1)-m_P(0)=\sum_{\ell=1}^{\beta} a_{P,\ell}.
\]
Thus the endpoint treatment contrast satisfies
\[
\tau_P=m_P(1)-m_P(0)=\sum_{\ell=1}^{\beta} a_{P,\ell}.
\]

08ffe7b5cadd617b04e9546d94106f4b4a2be2b4.title: TV Envelope Design Bound
08ffe7b5cadd617b04e9546d94106f4b4a2be2b4.body:
Let \(n,k,\beta\) be integers, let \(q\in\mathbb R\), and let \(\sigma_0^2\in\mathbb R\). Fix a finite assignment space \(\Omega\), a finite design \(\pi\) on \(\Omega\), rollout assignment vectors \(Z_0,\ldots,Z_k\), round means \(\bar Y_0,\ldots,\bar Y_k\), a rollout mean curve \(m_P\), polynomial coefficients \(a_{P,\ell}\), and a schedule \(p=(p_0,\ldots,p_k)\). Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Order.)} \(1\le \beta\le k\).
\item \textbf{(Envelope scale.)} \(\sigma_0^2\ge 0\).
\item \textbf{(Schedule.)} \(p\in S_{k,q}\), in the sense of Definition~\ref{obj:def:budgeted-schedule-class}.
\item \textbf{(Law class.)} The design, rollout variables, round means, mean curve, coefficients, envelope scale, and schedule satisfy the rollout law-class restrictions \(\mathcal P_\beta\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:rollout-law-class}.
\end{itemize}
Then \(W_\beta(p)\), as defined in Definition~\ref{obj:def:unbiased-weight-set}, is nonempty. Moreover, for every \(w\in W_\beta(p)\),
\[
\mathbb E_\pi\!\left[\sum_{j=0}^k w_j\bar Y_j\right]=m_P(1)-m_P(0)
\]
and
\[
\operatorname{Var}_\pi\!\left(\sum_{j=0}^k w_j\bar Y_j\right)
\le
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\left(\sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|\right)^2 .
\]
For every such \(w\), there also exists a positive semidefinite \((k+1)\times(k+1)\) matrix \(\Gamma\) such that
\[
\Gamma_{jj}\le \frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\quad\text{for every }j=0,\ldots,k,
\]
and
\[
\sum_{i=0}^k\sum_{j=0}^k w_i\Gamma_{ij}w_j
=
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\left(\sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|\right)^2 .
\]
Consequently, with \(A_\beta(\cdot)\) and \(M_{\beta,k,q}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:amplification-criterion},
\[
\inf\left\{
v:\ \exists w\in W_\beta(p)\text{ with }
v=\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\left(\sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|\right)^2
\right\}
=
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}A_\beta(p),
\]
and
\[
\inf\left\{
v:\ \exists p'\in S_{k,q}\text{ with }
v=\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}A_\beta(p')
\right\}
=
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}M_{\beta,k,q}.
\]
Finally, if \(p^\star\in S_{k,q}\) satisfies
\[
A_\beta(p^\star)=M_{\beta,k,q},
\]
then for every \(p'\in S_{k,q}\),
\[
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}A_\beta(p^\star)
\le
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}A_\beta(p').
\]

8d5b10a52f502cbd233040c1bc9e50586167b099.title: Exterior Chebyshev Extremal Bound
8d5b10a52f502cbd233040c1bc9e50586167b099.body:
Let $\beta$ be a positive integer. For every real polynomial $P$ and every exterior point $x_0$, suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Degree bound.)} $\deg(P)\le \beta$.
\item \textbf{(Unit interval bound.)} For every $x\in[-1,1]$, $|P(x)|\le 1$.
\item \textbf{(Exterior point.)} $x_0>1$.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
|P(x_0)|\le T_\beta(x_0),
\]
where $T_\beta$ is the Chebyshev polynomial of the first kind of degree $\beta$.

fdd4d8dc4cb4e2706f00805e610cd9871b282f43.title: Dual Norm Amplification
fdd4d8dc4cb4e2706f00805e610cd9871b282f43.body:
Let \(\beta,k\in\mathbb N\) and let \(p=(p_0,\ldots,p_k)\) be a real-valued schedule whose nodes are distinct. Suppose that the unbiased weight set \(W_\beta(p)\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:unbiased-weight-set} is nonempty. Then
\[
\inf_{w\in W_\beta(p)} \sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|
=
\sup\left\{
|r(1)-r(0)|:
r\in\mathbb R[x],\ \deg(r)\le \beta,\ \max_{0\le j\le k}|r(p_j)|\le 1
\right\}.
\]
Consequently, for the amplification criterion \(A_\beta(p)\) in Definition~\ref{obj:def:amplification-criterion},
\[
A_\beta(p)
=
\left(
\sup\left\{
|r(1)-r(0)|:
r\in\mathbb R[x],\ \deg(r)\le \beta,\ \max_{0\le j\le k}|r(p_j)|\le 1
\right\}
\right)^2 .
\]

94666aca82c8060f82243bf619b05d7a7222a285.title: Chebyshev Minimax Amplification
94666aca82c8060f82243bf619b05d7a7222a285.body:
Fix a budget cap \(q_{\max}\in(0,1)\). There exists a constant
\(C_-(q_{\max})>0\) such that, for every oversampling ratio \(c>1\), there
exists a constant \(C_+(c,q_{\max})>0\) with the following property.

For every pair of integers \(\beta,k\), every rollout budget \(q\), and every
schedule \(p\), suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Order and oversampling.)} \(\beta\ge 1\) and \(k\ge c\beta\).
\item \textbf{(Low-budget regime.)} \(q>0\) and the low-budget cap condition of
Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:low-budget-cap} holds for \((q,q_{\max})\).
\item \textbf{(Admissible schedule.)} When \(p\) is used below, \(p\in S_{k,q}\)
as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:budgeted-schedule-class}.
\end{itemize}
Then, with \(A_\beta(\cdot)\) and \(M_{\beta,k,q}\) as in
Definition~\ref{obj:def:amplification-criterion} and
\(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:chebyshev-schedule},
\[
A_\beta(p)
\ge
C_-(q_{\max})
\left(\frac{(1+\sqrt{1-q})^2}{q}\right)^{2\beta},
\]
and
\[
A_\beta\!\left(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\right)
\le
C_+(c,q_{\max})
\left(\frac{(1+\sqrt{1-q})^2}{q}\right)^{2\beta}.
\]
Moreover, the minimax amplification satisfies the matching two-sided bound
\[
C_-(q_{\max})
\left(\frac{(1+\sqrt{1-q})^2}{q}\right)^{2\beta}
\le
M_{\beta,k,q}
\le
C_+(c,q_{\max})
\left(\frac{(1+\sqrt{1-q})^2}{q}\right)^{2\beta}.
\]

c62f15ae53fe967407ee511a204d0ff9cba2458f.title: Oversampled Lobatto Norming
c62f15ae53fe967407ee511a204d0ff9cba2458f.body:
Suppose the Ehlich--Zeller Chebyshev--Lobatto mesh inequality holds: for every pair of nonnegative integers $\beta,k$ and every real polynomial $R$ with $\deg(R)\le \beta$ and $\beta<k$,
\[
 |R(x)|
 \le
 \frac{1}{\cos(\pi\beta/(2k))}
 \max_{0\le j\le k}\left|R\!\left(-\cos\!\left(\frac{\pi j}{k}\right)\right)\right|
\]
for every $x\in[-1,1]$.  Then, for every real oversampling ratio $c$ with $c>1$, there exists a real constant $K(c)>0$ such that the following holds.  For every pair of nonnegative integers $\beta,k$ and every real polynomial $R$ satisfying
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive order.)} $\beta\ge 1$,
\item \textbf{(Oversampling.)} $k\ge c\beta$,
\item \textbf{(Degree bound.)} $\deg(R)\le \beta$,
\end{itemize}
one has, for every $x\in[-1,1]$,
\[
 |R(x)|
 \le
 K(c)
 \max_{0\le j\le k}\left|R\!\left(-\cos\!\left(\frac{\pi j}{k}\right)\right)\right|.
\]

d36eff49bc0f37da3c768dfa1da2da0ba268ed48.title: Chebyshev Schedule Admissibility
d36eff49bc0f37da3c768dfa1da2da0ba268ed48.body: For every integer $k \ge 1$ and every $q \in (0,1]$, the shifted Chebyshev--Lobatto schedule $p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)$ of Definition~\ref{obj:def:chebyshev-schedule} is an element of the budgeted schedule class $S_{k,q}$ of Definition~\ref{obj:def:budgeted-schedule-class}. Equivalently, its coordinates all lie in $[0,1]$, it starts at $0$, is strictly increasing across the rollout measurement indices, and ends at $q$.

5d79212905f248e4bd9812c2d4b032fd77c2038b.title: Continuous Chebyshev Endpoint Bound
5d79212905f248e4bd9812c2d4b032fd77c2038b.body:
Fix \(q_{\max}\) with \(0<q_{\max}<1\). Then there exist constants \(C(q_{\max})>0\) and \(c(q_{\max})>0\) such that, for every \(\beta\in\mathbb N\) and every \(q\in\mathbb R\), if
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Degree.)} \(1\le \beta\),
\item \textbf{(Budget.)} \(0<q\le q_{\max}\),
\item \textbf{(Exterior notation.)} \(x_q=2/q-1\) and \(\lambda(q)=x_q+\sqrt{x_q^2-1}\),
\end{itemize}
then both of the following hold:
\[
\text{for every real polynomial }R\text{ with }\deg(R)\le \beta
\text{ and }\sup_{x\in[-1,1]}|R(x)|\le 1,\qquad
|R(x_q)-R(-1)|\le C(q_{\max})\,\lambda(q)^\beta,
\]
and
\[
T_\beta(x_q)-1\ge c(q_{\max})\,\lambda(q)^\beta .
\]

b1ce9325f5f73f1213f8857946d0ea54b15c2cc8.title: Nonempty Unbiased Weights
b1ce9325f5f73f1213f8857946d0ea54b15c2cc8.body:
Let \(q\in\mathbb R\), let \(\beta\) and \(k\) be integers, and let \(p=(p_0,\ldots,p_k)\) be a rollout schedule. Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Order.)} \(\beta\ge 1\).
\item \textbf{(Enough nodes.)} \(k\ge \beta\).
\item \textbf{(Budgeted schedule.)} \(p\in S_{k,q}\), with \(S_{k,q}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:budgeted-schedule-class}.
\end{itemize}
Then the unbiased weight set \(W_\beta(p)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:unbiased-weight-set} is nonempty. Equivalently, there exists a weight vector \(w=(w_0,\ldots,w_k)\) such that \(w\in W_\beta(p)\).

3aebfdb13d6b2d147fd2df0f554c5a944eb11766.title: Sharp Variance Envelope
3aebfdb13d6b2d147fd2df0f554c5a944eb11766.body:
For any population size $n$, final rollout index $k$, finite assignment space $\Omega$ with rollout randomization $\pi$, rollout-round statistics $X_0,\ldots,X_k$, variance scale $\sigma_0^2$, and weight vector $w=(w_0,\ldots,w_k)$, suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Nonnegative scale.)} $\sigma_0^2\ge 0$.
\item \textbf{(Round variance envelope.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:round-mean-variance-envelope} holds for $X_0,\ldots,X_k$ with scale $\sigma_0^2/n$.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
\operatorname{Var}_\pi\!\left(\sum_{j=0}^k w_j X_j\right)
\le
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\left(\sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|\right)^2 .
\]
Moreover, there exists a positive semidefinite matrix
$\Gamma\in\mathbb R^{(k+1)\times(k+1)}$ such that
\[
\Gamma_{jj}\le \frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\quad\text{for every }j=0,\ldots,k,
\]
and
\[
\sum_{i=0}^k\sum_{j=0}^k w_i\,\Gamma_{ij}\,w_j
=
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\left(\sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|\right)^2 .
\]

4736f717aa137a071e8405f43b42379e8b9b98de.title: Exact Risk Envelope Bound
4736f717aa137a071e8405f43b42379e8b9b98de.body:
Let $\Omega$ be a finite assignment space equipped with rollout randomization $\pi$. For any population size $n$, any integers $k$ and $\beta$, any real numbers $q$ and $\sigma_0^2$, and any schedule $p=(p_0,\ldots,p_k)$, suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Order.)} $\beta\ge 1$.
\item \textbf{(Number of rollout intervals.)} $k\ge \beta$.
\item \textbf{(Budget.)} $0<q\le 1$.
\item \textbf{(Variance scale.)} $\sigma_0^2\ge 0$.
\item \textbf{(Budgeted schedule.)} $p\in S_{k,q}$, as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:budgeted-schedule-class}.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
\inf_{w\in W_\beta(p)}
\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_\beta}
w^\prime \Gamma_P(p) w
\le
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\,A_\beta(p),
\]
with $W_\beta(p)$ as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:unbiased-weight-set}, $\mathcal P_\beta$ as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:rollout-law-class}, and $A_\beta(p)$ as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:amplification-criterion}. Consequently,
\[
R_{\mathrm{exact}}(\beta,k,q)
\le
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\,M_{\beta,k,q},
\]
where $R_{\mathrm{exact}}(\beta,k,q)$ is as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:exact-nested-risk} and $M_{\beta,k,q}$ is as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:amplification-criterion}.

a6334a545609f89e32ca9a281b86bb65ebf223c5.title: Feasible Chebyshev Rate
a6334a545609f89e32ca9a281b86bb65ebf223c5.body:
Fix an oversampling constant \(c\) and a budget cap \(q_{\max}\) such that \(c>1\) and \(0<q_{\max}<1\). Then there exists a constant \(C_+(c,q_{\max})>0\) such that, for every population size \(n\), polynomial order \(\beta\), number of rollout intervals \(k\), finite assignment space \(\Omega\), finite design \(\pi\) on \(\Omega\), rollout budget \(q\), and variance-envelope scale \(\sigma_0^2\), the following conditions imply the stated bound:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Order.)} \(\beta\ge 1\).
\item \textbf{(Budget.)} \(q>0\) and \(q\) satisfies the low-budget cap with \(q_{\max}\) in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:low-budget-cap}.
\item \textbf{(Variance scale.)} \(\sigma_0^2\ge 0\).
\item \textbf{(Chebyshev size.)} \(k=\lceil c\beta\rceil\).
\item \textbf{(Schedule.)} \(p=p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\) is the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule of Definition~\ref{obj:def:chebyshev-schedule}.
\end{itemize}
Under these conditions,
\[
\inf_{w\in W_\beta(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q))}
\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_\beta}
\operatorname{Var}_\pi\!\left(\sum_{j=0}^k w_j\bar Y_j\right)
\le
\frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}\,
C_+(c,q_{\max})
\left(\frac{(1+\sqrt{1-q})^2}{q}\right)^{2\beta},
\]
where \(W_\beta(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q))\) is the unbiased weight set of Definition~\ref{obj:def:unbiased-weight-set}, and the supremum ranges over rollout laws \(P\in\mathcal P_\beta\) satisfying Definition~\ref{obj:def:rollout-law-class} at the schedule \(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\) with variance-envelope scale \(\sigma_0^2\).

581ed59fa34f79122090a43046f225a366637431.title: Static Rollout Consistency
581ed59fa34f79122090a43046f225a366637431.body:
At each rollout measurement \(j\), the observed round mean is
\[
\bar Y_j
=
n^{-1}\sum_{i\in U_n} Y_i(Z_j).
\]
The potential outcomes \(Y_i(Z_j)\) depend on the contemporaneous assignment vector \(Z_j\), but not on earlier rollout steps.

e88ec32cf875af157e32ccc0c9cbf8a853a3db11.title: Polynomial Rollout Mean
e88ec32cf875af157e32ccc0c9cbf8a853a3db11.body:
For every law \(P\), the rollout mean curve satisfies
\[
m_P(u)=\sum_{\ell=0}^{\beta} a_{P,\ell}u^\ell
\]
for all \(u\in[0,1]\).

369957eebb1db2115da14d6c5aa0ebff9fe3c213.title: Round-Mean Variance Envelope
369957eebb1db2115da14d6c5aa0ebff9fe3c213.body:
For every law \(P\), every admissible schedule \(p\), and every rollout measurement \(j\),
\[
\operatorname{Var}_\pi(\bar Y_j)\le \frac{\sigma_0^2}{n}.
\]

0c93e7104c5f134866e7127e87f10616be3d6949.title: Low-Budget Cap
0c93e7104c5f134866e7127e87f10616be3d6949.body:
The rollout budget and its cap satisfy
\[
q\le q_{\max}<1.
\]

604309490eabdf0ffbb64c2a55a7a75cb5d1bcea.title: Budgeted schedule class \(S_{k,q}\)
604309490eabdf0ffbb64c2a55a7a75cb5d1bcea.body:
The budgeted schedule class is
\[
S_{k,q}
=
\left\{
p\in[0,1]^{k+1}:
p_0=0
\ \text{and}\
0<p_1<\cdots<p_k=q
\right\}.
\]

3d99c08556bb38aee66cb21605c185b0db457ea4.title: Rollout law class \(\mathcal P_\beta\)
3d99c08556bb38aee66cb21605c185b0db457ea4.body:
The rollout law class is
\[
\mathcal P_\beta
=
\left\{
P:
P \text{ satisfies Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:static-rollout-consistency}, \ref{obj:ass:beta-order-polynomial}, and \ref{obj:ass:round-mean-variance-envelope}}
\right\}.
\]

82ec539317f6cc0ac7ee382502412ddac1a97489.title: Unbiased weight set $W_\beta(p)$
82ec539317f6cc0ac7ee382502412ddac1a97489.body:
For a schedule \(p=(p_0,\ldots,p_k)\), the set of unbiased weights of order \(\beta\) is
\[
W_\beta(p)
=
\left\{
w\in\mathbb R^{k+1}:
\sum_{j=0}^k w_j p_j^0=0
\ \text{and}\
\sum_{j=0}^k w_j p_j^\ell=1
\ \text{for every } \ell=1,\ldots,\beta
\right\}.
\]

1429f80288c30933b51c185e408ab82bee6ca291.title: Chebyshev schedule \(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\)
1429f80288c30933b51c185e408ab82bee6ca291.body:
For \(j=0,\ldots,k\), the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto node on \([0,q]\) is
\[
p^{\mathrm{Ch}}_j(k,q)
=
\frac{q}{2}\left\{1-\cos\left(\frac{\pi j}{k}\right)\right\}.
\]
The shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule is
\[
p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)
=
\left(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}_0(k,q),\ldots,p^{\mathrm{Ch}}_k(k,q)\right).
\]

3bfe0027b2b8f9c07ecc9229bca539980e2542f6.title: Amplification criterion \(A_\beta(p)\)
3bfe0027b2b8f9c07ecc9229bca539980e2542f6.body:
For a schedule \(p\), the amplification criterion is
\[
A_\beta(p)
=
\inf_{w\in W_\beta(p)}
\left(\sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|\right)^2.
\]
The minimax amplification over budgeted schedules is
\[
M_{\beta,k,q}
=
\inf_{p\in S_{k,q}} A_\beta(p).
\]

f9ddbf1538f215f0bff545484d333920a6edf360.title: Exact nested risk \(R_{\mathrm{exact}}(\beta,k,q)\)

a0b659c116941b2a4e24874314fb92732647ed4f.title: Exact nested optimality
a0b659c116941b2a4e24874314fb92732647ed4f.body:
Fix a budget cap \(q_{\max}\), let \(\beta\ge 1\), set \(k=\lceil c\beta\rceil\), and take \(q\in(0,q_{\max}]\). Suppose that the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule \(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\) is admissible, so that
\[
p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\in S_{k,q}.
\]
For the exact finite-population nested-rollout minimax problem
\[
R_{\mathrm{exact}}(\beta,k,q)
=
\inf_{p\in S_{k,q}}
\inf_{w\in W_\beta(p)}
\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_\beta}
w^\prime \Gamma_P(p) w,
\]
where \(\Gamma_P(p)\) is the true covariance matrix of the monotone Bernoulli rollout, the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule \(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\) is rate-feasible. Whether \(p^{\mathrm{Ch}}(k,q)\) attains the exact nested risk \(R_{\mathrm{exact}}(\beta,k,q)\) is open.

617e153c12c2ba4345d07ba0a7c0a79cea1934b3.detail: p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,q) [undefined] in prop:equal-spacing-benchmark/prop:no-extrapolation-boundary — add a definitionv for the equal-spacing schedule p^{\mathrm{eq}}(\beta,q) and reference it from both equal-spacing lemmas

T_\beta [undefined] in lem:chebyshev-exterior-extremal/lem:continuous-chebyshev-endpoint-bound — add a definitionv for the Chebyshev polynomial T_\beta and reference it from the two Chebyshev endpoint/extremal lemmas

round variance envelope for X_0,\ldots,X_k [mismatch] in lem:variance-envelope-sharpness — either specialize the lemma to the round means \bar Y_j or add a separate anchored generic variance-envelope predicate for arbitrary statistics X_0,\ldots,X_k

monotone Bernoulli rollout [undefined] in oeq:exact-nested-minimax — add an anchored definition of the monotone Bernoulli rollout law or remove this qualifier from the exact nested optimality statement
rate-feasible [undefined] in oeq:exact-nested-minimax — define the predicate rate-feasible in a definitionv or replace the phrase with the explicit bound it is meant to assert

R_{\mathrm{exact}}(\beta,k,q) [mismatch] in oeq:exact-nested-minimax — make oeq:exact-nested-minimax refer to Definition~\ref{obj:def:exact-nested-risk} instead of restating R_{\mathrm{exact}} with the extra undefined monotone-Bernoulli covariance qualifier
